title: Protocol for Use with Standard Insert Libraries (370-420 bp)
author: New England Biolabs

steps.title: DNA and Control DNA Combine genomic...
steps.instruction: DNA and Control DNA Combine genomic DNA (10–200 ng) with control DNAs specified below. Genomic DNA can be in any of the following buffers:10 mM Tris pH 8.0, 1X TE (10mM Tris pH 8.0, 1mM EDTA), or low TE (10 mM Tris pH 8.0, 0.1 mM EDTA). A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 gDNA 48 µl 3 (lilac) Control DNA Unmethylated Lambda (see Table 1.1) 1 µl 4 (lilac) Control DNA CpG methylated pUC19 (see Table 1.1) 1 µl 5 Total Volume 50 µl The following table is a guide for the amount of (lilac) Control DNA CpG methylated pUC19 and (lilac) Control DNA Unmethylated Lambda DNA to be added to samples prior to EM-seq library construction. Expected read numbers along with read length should be considered to ensure that enough controls are included for the users individual sequencing goals. Table 1.1 Dilutions of control DNAs for a range of genomic DNA inputs. These are suitable for shallow/pre-sequencing (approx. 2 – 4 million paired reads) on a MiSeq prior to deep sequencing (approx. 100-150 million paired reads) on NovaSeq, HiSeq or NextSeq. A B C 1 DILUTION OF (LILAC) LAMBDA CONTROL AND (LILAC) pUC19 CONTROL 2 DNA Input Amount Pre-sequencing 2-4 Milliion Paired Reads Deep Sequencing 100-150 Million Paired Reads 3 10 ng 1:20 1:100 4 200 ng No Dilution 1:50 Regardless of sequencing depth, a minimum of 5,000 paired end reads with a read length of 76 bases, for unmethylated Lambda DNA, and 500 paired end reads with a read length of 76 bases, for CpG methylated pUC19, are needed to give enough coverage for accurate conversion estimates. Different applications may require different sequencing depths and therefore different strategies should be employed when deciding how much control DNA should be added. For example, some libraries may only need 2 million paired end reads whereas other may require 50 million paired end reads or even 300 million paired end reads. Additional considerations, regarding the amount of controls added, should be taken into account. For example, pre-sequencing libraries to a depth of 2-4 million paired end reads using the recommended dilution for the controls (Table 1.1), followed by deeper sequencing of these same libraries to a higher depth of 100 – 150 million paired reads per library would result in excess reads associated with the controls. This strategy is recommended users who choose to check library conversion prior to deepersequencing. Users who dilute controls based on pre-sequencing guidelines will have excess control reads, thus ensuring a higher confidence in library conversion.

steps.title: On ice , mix the following...
steps.instruction: On ice , mix the following components in a sterile nuclease-free PCR tube: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Sheared DNA (Step 2) 50 µl 3 (green) NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 7 µl 4 (green) NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 3 µl 5 Total Volume 60 µl Note: NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer and Enzyme Mix can be pre-mixed ahead of time as a master mix.

steps.title: On ice , add the following...
steps.instruction: On ice , add the following components directly to the End Prep reaction mixture and mix well: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 End Prep reaction mixture (Step 5) 60 µl 3 (red) NEBNext EM-seq Adaptor 2.5 µl 4 (red) NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 1 µl 5 (red) NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 30 µl 6 Total Volume 93.5 µl Note: Ligation Enhancer and Ligation Master Mix can be mixed ahead of time and is stable for at least 8 hours at 4 °C . We do not recommend adding adaptor to a premix in the adaptor ligation step. Premix adaptor and sample and then add the other ligation reagents.

steps.title: Set a 100 µL or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 µL or 200 µL pipette to 80 µL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. CAUTION: The Ligation Master Mix is viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Place in a thermal cycler, and...
steps.instruction: Place in a thermal cycler, and run the following program with the heated lid off: 15 minutes at 20°CHold at 4°C Safe Stopping Point: Samples can be stored overnight at -20°C.

steps.title: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 110 µl (~1
steps.instruction: Add 110 µl (~1.1X) of resuspended NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to each sample. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix.

steps.title: Incubate samples on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the tubes against an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes against an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution...
steps.instruction: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. CAUTION: DO NOT discard the beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tubes while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 and then carefully removing and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: Repeat the ethanol wash once for...
steps.instruction: Repeat the ethanol wash once for a total of two washes. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash using a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 2 minutes while the tubes are on the magnetic stand with the lid open. CAUTION: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 29 µL, of Elution Buffer (white).

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times. Incubate for at least 00:01:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic stand. After 00:03:00 , or whenever the solution is clear, transfer 28 µL, of the supernatant to a new PCR tube. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: Prepare TET2 Buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare TET2 Buffer . Use option A if you have E7120S/ E7120G (24 Reactions/G size) and option B if you have E7120L (96 reactions). The TET2 Reaction Buffer Supplement is a powder. Centrifuge before use to ensure it is at the bottom of the tube .

steps.title: On ice , add the following...
steps.instruction: On ice , add the following components directly to the EM-seq adaptor ligated DNA. A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 EM-seq adaptor ligated DNA (Step 19) 28 µl 3 (yellow) TET2 Reaction Buffer (TET2 Reaction Buffer plus reconstituted TET2 Reaction Buffer Supplement) 10 µl 4 (yellow) Oxidation Supplement 1 µl 5 (yellow) DTT 1 µl 6 (yellow) Oxidation Enhancer 1 µl 7 (yellow) TET2 4 µl 8 Total Volume 45 µl Mix thoroughly by vortexing, centrifuge briefly. For multiple reactions, a master mix of the above reaction components can be prepared before addition to the sample DNA. 5mC/5hmC oxidation is initiated by the addition of the Fe(II) solution to the reaction in the next step.

steps.title: Dilute the 500 mM (yellow) Fe(II)...
steps.instruction: Dilute the 500 mM (yellow) Fe(II) Solution by adding 1 µL to 1249 µL, of water . Note: The 500 mM (yellow) Fe(II) solution color can vary between colorless to yellow, this is normal. Use the diluted solution immediately, do not store it. Discard after use. Combine Diluted (yellow) Fe(II) Solution and Reaction DNA Oxidation Enzymes as described below. A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Reaction Mixture (Step 23) 45 μl 3 Diluted Fe(II) Solution (from step 24) 5 μl 4 Total Volume 50 μl Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times, centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Place in a thermal cycler, and...
steps.instruction: Place in a thermal cycler, and run the following program with the heated lid set to ≥ 45°C or on:1 hour at 37°CHold at 4°C

steps.title: Transfer the samples to ice and...
steps.instruction: Transfer the samples to ice and add 1 µL, of Stop Reagent (yellow). A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Oxidized DNA (Step 25) 50 µl 3 (yellow) Stop Reagent 1 µl 4 Total Volume 51 µl Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Place in a thermal cycler, and...
steps.instruction: Place in a thermal cycler, and run the following program with the heated lid set to ≥45°C or on: 30 minutes at 37°CHold at 4°C SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored overnight at either 4 °C in the thermal cycler or at -20 °C in the freezer.

steps.title: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 90 µl (1
steps.instruction: Add 90 µl (1.8X) of resuspended NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to each sample. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix.

steps.title: Incubate samples on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the tubes against an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes against an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution...
steps.instruction: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution is clear) carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. CAUTION: DO NOT discard the beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tubes while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash using a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 2 minutes while the tubes are on the magnetic stand with the lid open. CAUTION: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 17 µL, of Elution Buffer (white) .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times. Incubate for at least 00:01:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic stand. After 00:03:00 (or whenever the solution is clear), transfer 16 µL, of the supernatant to a new PCR tube. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored Overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: Caution
steps.instruction: Caution: The DNA can be denatured using either Formamide or 0.1 N Sodium Hydroxide. Use Option A for denaturing using Formamide and Option B for denaturing using 0.1 N Sodium hydroxide.

steps.title: Place in a thermal cycler with...
steps.instruction: Place in a thermal cycler with the heated lid set to ≥ 75 °C or on, and run the following program: 00:30:00 at 20 °C 00:30:00 at 65 °C Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Set a 100 µL or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 µL or 200 µL pipette to 50 µL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Note: It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with the performance.

steps.title: The combined 50 µl genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: The combined 50 µl genomic DNA and control DNAs are fragmented to an average fragment size of ~300 bp (370–420 bp final Illumina library). Fragmentation can be done using a preferred fragmentation device such as a Covaris instrument. Enzymatic fragmentation is not recommended as this may result in the removal of methylation marks. Transfer the 50 µl of sheared DNA to a new PCR tube for End Prep. Note: DNA does not need to be cleaned up or size selected before End Prep

steps.title: Option A
steps.instruction: Option A: E7120S/E7120G Add 100 µL, of TET2 Reaction Buffer to one tube of TET2 Reaction Buffer Supplement and mix well. Write date on tube.

steps.title: Option B
steps.instruction: Option B: E7120L Add 400 µL, of TET2 Reaction Buffer to one tube of TET2 Reaction Buffer Supplement and mix well. Write date on tube. The reconstituted buffer should be stored at -20 °C and discarded after 4 months.

steps.title: Use a Bioanalyzer or TapeStation to...
steps.instruction: Use a Bioanalyzer or TapeStation to determine the size distribution and concentration of the libraries. A typical EM-seq library would have the following TapeStation trace. Sequence using the preferred Illumina platform. 2 x 76 base reads or 2 x 100 base reads for standard sized libraries.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic stand. After 00:03:00 (or whenever the solution is clear), transfer 20 µL, of the supernatant to a new PCR tube. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times. Incubate for at least 00:01:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 21 µL, of Elution Buffer (white) For long terms storage use, 21 µl of 1XTE (10 mM Tris, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0), Low TE (10 mM Tris, 0.2 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) or 0.1XTE (1 mM Tris, 0.1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0).

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 00:02:00 while the tubes are on the magnetic stand with the lid open. CAUTION: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry.

steps.title: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash using a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tubes while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution...
steps.instruction: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. CAUTION: DO NOT discard the beads.

steps.title: Place the tubes against an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes against an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate samples on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 45 µl (0
steps.instruction: Add 45 µl (0.9X) of resuspended NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to each sample. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix.

steps.title: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: DenPlace the tube in a thermal...
steps.instruction: DenPlace the tube in a thermal cycler with the heated lid set to 105°C and perform PCR amplification using the following cycling conditions: A B C D 1 CYCLE STEP TEMP TIME CYCLES 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 1 3 Denaturation 98°C 10 seconds 4-8* 4 Annealing 62°C 30 seconds 5 Extension 65°C 60 seconds 6 Final Extension 65°C 5 minutes 1 7 Hold 4°C ∞ - *Cycle Recommendations: 10 ng DNA input: 8 cycles 50 ng DNA input: 5-6 cycles 200 ng DNA input: 4 cycles

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times, centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic stand. After 00:03:00 (or whenever the solution is clear), transfer 20 µL, of the supernatant to a new PCR tube. SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored Overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times. Incubate for at least 00:01:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube before placing back on the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 21 µL, of Elution Buffer (white) .

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 00:01:30 while the tubes are on the magnetic stand with the lid open. CAUTION: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry.

steps.title: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash once for a total of two washes. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash using a p10 pipette tip.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, of 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tubes while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

steps.title: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution...
steps.instruction: After 00:05:00 (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. CAUTION: DO NOT discard the beads.

steps.title: Place the tubes against an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes against an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate sample on bench top for...
steps.instruction: Incubate sample on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 100 µl (1
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl (1.0X) of resuspended NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to each sample. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix.

steps.title: CAUTION
steps.instruction: CAUTION: The Sample Purification Beads behave differently during the APOBEC clean-up. After the bead washes, do not overdry the beads as they become very difficult to resuspend. Vortex Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Place in a thermal cycler, and...
steps.instruction: Place in a thermal cycler, and run the following program wiht the heated lid set to ≥ 45 °C or on. 03:00:00 at 37 °C Hold at 4 °C SAFE STOPPING POINT: Samples can be stored overnight at either 4 °C in the thermal cycler or at -20 °C in the freezer.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by vortexing or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times, centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: On ice , add the following...
steps.instruction: On ice , add the following components to the denatured DNA. A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Denatured DNA (Step 43 or 48) 20 µl 3 Nuclease-free water 68 µl 4 (orange) APOBEC Reaction Buffer 10 µl 5 (orange) BSA 1 µl 6 (orange) APOBEC 1 µl 7 Total Volume 100 µl For multiple reactions, a master mix of the reaction components can be prepared before addition to the denatured DNA.

steps.title: Pre-heat thermal cycler to 85 °C...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat thermal cycler to 85 °C with the heated lid set to ≥ 105°C or on.

steps.title: Add 4 µL, Formamide to the...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL, Formamide to the 16 µL, of oxidized DNA (Step 39). Vortex to mix or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times, centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at 85 °C for 00:10:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 85 °C for 00:10:00 in the pre-heated thermal cycler.

steps.title: Immediately place On ice and allow...
steps.instruction: Immediately place On ice and allow the sample to fully cool (~2 minutes) before proceeding to Deamination of Cytosines.

steps.title: Prepare freshly diluted 0
steps.instruction: Prepare freshly diluted 0.1 N NaOH.

steps.title: Pre-heat thermal cycler to 50 °C...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat thermal cycler to 50 °C with the heated lid set to ≥ 105 °C or on.

steps.title: Add 4 µL, 0
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL, 0.1 N NaOH to the 16 µL, of oxidized DNA (Step 39). Vortex to mix or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times, centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at 50 °C for 00:10:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50 °C for 00:10:00 in the pre-heated thermal cycler.

steps.title: Immediately place On ice and allow...
steps.instruction: Immediately place On ice and allow the sample to fully cool (~2 minutes) before proceeding to Deamination of Cytosines.

steps.title: On ice, add the following components to the deaminated DNA
steps.instruction: On ice, add the following components to the deaminated DNA: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Deaminated DNA (Step 62) 20 µl 3 EM-seq Index Primer*, ** 5 µl 4 (blue) NEBNext Q5U Master Mix 25 µl 5 Total Volume 50 µl *Refer to Section 3 in the manual at NEB.com for barcode pooling guidelines. **EM-seq primers are supplied in tubes in NEB #E7120S or as a 96 Unique Dual Index Primers Pairs Plate in NEB #E7120L.
